remember: Recall the causes and symptoms of various human diseases
Explain the role of pathogens in disease causation
Identify the types of immunity and their functions
List the major types of human diseases and their characteristics
understand: Describe the basic principles of disease transmission and prevention
Explain the concept of health and disease, including the social and environmental factors that influence them
Discuss the role of human behavior and lifestyle in disease prevention and management
Understand the importance of sanitation, hygiene, and personal hygiene in disease prevention
apply: Apply knowledge of disease transmission and prevention to real-world scenarios
Design a plan for disease prevention and management based on knowledge of human behavior and lifestyle
Use knowledge of immunology to explain the mechanisms of vaccine development and use
Analyze the impact of environmental factors on human health and disease
analyze: Analyze the role of genetics and epigenetics in disease susceptibility and progression
Evaluate the effectiveness of different disease prevention and management strategies
Compare and contrast different types of human diseases and their characteristics
Synthesize knowledge of human health and disease to develop a comprehensive understanding of human health and well-being

ncert_objectives: Describe the basic principles of disease transmission and prevention.
Explain the role of human behavior and lifestyle in disease prevention and management.
Discuss the importance of sanitation, hygiene, and personal hygiene in disease prevention.
Describe the causes and symptoms of various human diseases.
board_exam_objectives: Disease: Definition, causes and symptoms, transmission and prevention.
Immunity: Types and functions, vaccine and vaccination.
Health and disease: Concept, health and disease, social and environmental factors.
